text.title: event-sourcing-basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the event-sourcing-basics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Once written, an event is never modified or deleted. Replays must produce identical state given the same event sequence.
rule.rationale: Mutable events destroy the audit trail and break replay determinism.

rule.statement: Events name what HAPPENED in past tense (OrderPlaced, PaymentCaptured). Never imperative (PlaceOrder is a command, not an event).
Apply via dispatch (_apply / handle_<EventClass>); never inline state mutation in command handlers.
rule.rationale: Inline mutation skips replay, breaks audit-trail equivalence.

rule.statement: Version increments by 1 per event applied. Concurrent appends use optimistic concurrency on expected_version.
rule.rationale: Optimistic concurrency is the only safe append model for an event-sourced stream.

rule.statement: Event schema evolution is planned upfront: additive fields with sensible defaults; new event versions get new class names (OrderPlacedV2).
rule.rationale: Replays cross historical schemas; ad-hoc evolution breaks them.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
